headlines.title: Apple to let users choose rival AI models across iOS 27 features
headlines.description: Google and Anthropic integrations tested so far.
headlines.summary: Apple will let iOS users pick third‑party AI models across system features.
headlines.why_it_matters: Opens a huge new distribution channel for model providers and changes mobile AI integration.

headlines.title: Apple Plans to Turn Devices Into AI Platforms
headlines.description: Apple plans to offer third-party artificial intelligence models alongside its own in-house models, Bloomberg reported Tuesday (May 5), citing unnamed sources. With this offering, users of the company’s software will be able to choose the model they want to us…
headlines.summary: Apple plans to turn its devices into platforms hosting both in‑house and external AI models.
headlines.why_it_matters: Signals a major new AI ecosystem and deployment surface for apps and model vendors.

headlines.title: Google, Microsoft and xAI agree to allow government safety checks of their AI models prior to release
headlines.description: Google LLC, Microsoft Corp. and xAI have agreed to share unreleased versions of their artificial intelligence models with the U.S. Department of Commerce to ensure the technologies do not pose a threat. Today, the Center for AI Standards and Innovation, CAISI…
headlines.summary: Google, Microsoft and xAI agreed to pre‑release safety checks of their AI models by Commerce.
headlines.why_it_matters: Shows how US regulation and government testing will shape model release processes and requirements.

headlines.title: Commerce AI center will evaluate Google Deepmind, Microsoft and xAI models
headlines.description: A renegotiated deal between the three companies and the Center for Artificial Intelligence Standards and Innovation allows private sector models to undergo safety testing in classified environments.
headlines.summary: A new Commerce AI center will evaluate Google DeepMind, Microsoft and xAI models.
headlines.why_it_matters: Practitioners must anticipate standardized benchmarks, compliance checks and documentation for frontier models.

headlines.title: Meta Develops Easy-to-Use AI Agent for Consumers
headlines.description: Meta plans to build an agentic artificial intelligence assistant for consumers that is comparable to OpenClaw but easier to use, The Financial Times (FT) reported Tuesday (May 5), citing unnamed sources. The assistant is being trialed internally by some staff…
headlines.summary: Meta is building an easy‑to‑use consumer AI agent similar to OpenAI’s assistants.
headlines.why_it_matters: Raises the bar for agentic assistants and offers a massive consumer deployment channel.

headlines.title: Anthropic CEO Predicts Firms Have 6 Months to Patch Software Vulnerabilities
headlines.description: Financial services companies and other organizations have six to 12 months to fix vulnerabilities in their software before Chinese artificial intelligence models develop capabilities equal to those of Anthropic’s Mythos, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said Tuesda…
headlines.summary: Anthropic’s CEO warned firms have 6–12 months to fix software before AI‑driven exploits surge.
headlines.why_it_matters: Highlights urgent need to harden codebases as AI‑powered vulnerability discovery accelerates attacks.

headlines.title: AI data center operator IREN to acquire Mirantis for $625M
headlines.description: Artificial intelligence data center operator IREN Ltd. today announced plans to acquire Mirantis Inc., a provider of infrastructure management software. The all-stock transaction is worth $625 million. Mirantis previously raised about $220 million in funding …
headlines.summary: AI data center operator IREN will acquire infrastructure software firm Mirantis for $625 million.
headlines.why_it_matters: Underscores consolidation of compute and cloud‑native tooling critical for large‑scale AI deployment.

headlines.title: ElevenLabs adds high-profile investors after annualized revenue tops $500M
headlines.description: ElevenLabs Inc., a developer of artificial intelligence models optimized for audio processing, today announced that several dozen investors have joined its latest funding round. The startup first closed the Series D investment in February with a $500 million …
headlines.summary: ElevenLabs added high‑profile investors after its annualized revenue passed $500 million.
headlines.why_it_matters: Validates strong market demand for AI audio tools and suggests growing competition in speech tech.

headlines.title: Arvind Krishna’s keynote at IBM Think: AI-first enterprises, hybrid as default, quantum moves from science to engineering
headlines.description: The artificial intelligence era is widening the gap between winners and laggards, and the delta is not only determined by who has the most AI but also how deeply AI is embedded into business processes. That’s according to IBM Corp. Chief Executive Arvind Kris…
headlines.summary: IBM’s CEO outlined an AI‑first, hybrid‑cloud and quantum roadmap at IBM Think.
headlines.why_it_matters: Signals how large enterprises will structure AI stacks, workloads and infrastructure choices.

headlines.title: A foundation model of vision, audition, and language for in-silico neuroscience
headlines.description: Cognitive neuroscience is fragmented into specialized models, each tailored to specific experimental paradigms, hence preventing a unified model of cognition in the human brain. Here, we introduce TRIBE v2, a tri-modal (video, audio and language) foundation m…
headlines.summary: Researchers released a foundation model unifying vision, audio and language for in‑silico neuroscience.
headlines.why_it_matters: Offers a rich multimodal model and dataset framework useful for brain‑inspired and cognitive AI research.

papers.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours
papers.abstract: Driven by a rapid co-evolution of both harness and underlying models, LLM agents are improving at a dizzying pace. In our prior work (performed in Dec. 2025), we introduced "Design Conductor" (or just "Conductor"), a system capable of building a 5-stage Linux-capable RISC-V CPU in 12 hours. In this work, we introduce an updated multi-agent harness powered by frontier models released in April 2026, which is able to handle 80x larger tasks, at higher quality, fully autonomously. Following a brief introduction, we examine 4 designs that the system produced autonomously, including "VerTQ", an LLM inference accelerator which hard-wires support for TurboQuant in a 240-cycle pipeline, starting from the TurboQuant arXiv paper. VerTQ includes heavy compute processing, with 5129 FP16/32 units; the design was mapped to an FPGA at 125 MHz and consumes 5.7 mm^2 in TSMC 16FF (8 attention pipes). We review the key new characteristics that enabled these results. Finally, we analyze Design Conductor's token usage and other empirical characteristics, including its limitations.
papers.summary: Shows an AI agent designing real computer hardware end‑to‑end.

papers.title: Rollout Pass-Rate Control: Steering Binary-Reward RL Toward Its Most Informative Regime
papers.abstract: SWE-bench-style agentic reinforcement learning relies on expensive stateful trajectories, yet substantial compute is wasted on sampled rollout groups with skewed pass rates, where binary rewards provide a weak contrastive signal. We frame this inefficiency as a pass-rate control problem and show that a 50% pass rate is the most informative operating point: it maximizes reward entropy, the probability of surviving group filtering, RLOO advantage energy under GRPO, and success--failure contrastive structure. Guided by this principle, we propose Prefix Sampling (PS), which replays trajectory prefixes to steer skewed groups toward this regime: successful prefixes serve as head starts for mostly failing groups, while failing prefixes serve as handicaps for mostly passing groups. In stateful agent environments, prefix states are reconstructed through replay while replayed tokens are excluded from the loss, restricting optimization to continuations generated by the current policy. On SWE-bench-style agentic RL, PS delivers end-to-end wall-clock speedups of 2.01x on Qwen3-14B and 1.55x on Qwen3-32B while preserving or improving final verified performance. For 14B, the SWE-bench Verified peak rises from the baseline peak of 0.273 to 0.295 under PS. Additional mathematical reasoning experiments on AIME 2025 show the same pass-rate control pattern and decompose the gains into replay, bidirectional coverage, and adaptive control.
papers.summary: Improves RL training efficiency for code‑fixing software agents.

papers.title: Continual Knowledge Updating in LLM Systems: Learning Through Multi-Timescale Memory Dynamics
papers.abstract: LLMs are trained once, then deployed into a world that never stops changing. External memory compensates for this, but most systems manage it explicitly rather than letting it adapt on its own. Biological memory works differently: coupled multi-timescale dynamics make new associations immediately usable, strengthen what repetition confirms, and let the rest fade. We argue that external memory should follow a similar principle. In Memini, this view takes the form of an associative memory that organizes knowledge as a directed graph. Each edge carries two coupled internal variables, one fast and one slow, following the Benna-Fusi model of synaptic consolidation. From this coupling, episodic sensitivity, gradual consolidation, and selective forgetting emerge as facets of a single mechanism, reframing external memory as a learning substrate that reorganizes through its own dynamics.
papers.summary: Proposes self‑updating memory systems for continually learning LLMs.

papers.title: Automatically Finding and Validating Unexpected Side-Effects of Interventions on Language Models
papers.abstract: We present an automated, contrastive evaluation pipeline for auditing the behavioral impact of interventions on large language models. Given a base model $M_1$ and an intervention model $M_2$, our method compares their free-form, multi-token generations across aligned prompt contexts and produces human-readable, statistically validated natural-language hypotheses describing how the models differ, along with recurring themes that summarize patterns across validated hypotheses.   We evaluate the approach in synthetic setting by injecting known behavioral changes and showing that the pipeline reliably recovers them. We then apply it to three real-world interventions, reasoning distillation, knowledge editing and unlearning, demonstrating that the method surfaces both intended and unexpected behavioral shifts, distinguishes large from subtle interventions, and does not hallucinate differences when effects are absent or misaligned with the prompt bank. Overall, the pipeline provides a statistically grounded and interpretable tool for post-hoc auditing of intervention-induced changes in model behavior.
papers.summary: Automatically finds hidden side‑effects of changing language models.

papers.title: The Impossibility Triangle of Long-Context Modeling
papers.abstract: We identify and prove a fundamental trade-off governing long-sequence models: no model can simultaneously achieve (i) per-step computation independent of sequence length (Efficiency), (ii) state size independent of sequence length (Compactness), and (iii) the ability to recall a number of historical facts proportional to sequence length (Recall). We formalize this trade-off within an Online Sequence Processor abstraction that unifies Transformers, state space models, linear recurrent networks, and their hybrids. Using the Data Processing Inequality and Fano's Inequality, we prove that any model satisfying Efficiency and Compactness can recall at most O(poly(d)/log V) key-value pairs from a sequence of arbitrary length, where d is the model dimension and V is the vocabulary size. We classify 52 architectures published before March 2026 into the triangle, showing that each achieves at most two of the three properties and that hybrid architectures trace continuous trajectories in the interior. Experiments on synthetic associative recall tasks with five representative architectures validate the theoretical bound: empirical recall capacity lies strictly below the information-theoretic limit, and no architecture escapes the triangle.
papers.summary: Proves a core trade‑off in long‑context model design.

project_ideas.title: 📱 AI Feature FOMO Detector
project_ideas.description: A tiny web app that compares your phone model to current AI features using an LLM and tells you, in plain language, what you’re “missing” (or not).

project_ideas.title: 🧠 Frankencitation Buster
project_ideas.description: A browser bookmarklet that sends highlighted text to an LLM and instantly flags likely AI-made ‘frankencitations’ with simple explanations.

project_ideas.title: 🛒 Smart Fridge Note Buddy
project_ideas.description: A simple chat-style grocery helper that turns your rough meal ideas into a clean shopping list using an LLM, ready to pin on your fridge or share.
